title: Several parameters are implicitly nullable, triggering deprecation warnings in PHP 8.4
author: redbeardcreator
body:
As it says in the subject. As an example, from `Poll.php`

```
Functional\poll(): Implicitly marking parameter $delaySequence as nullable is
deprecated, the explicit nullable type must be used instead in
vendor/lstrojny/functional-php/src/Functional/Poll.php on line 29.
```

I should be able to put together a pull request some time this weekend, as soon as I identify the other PHP 8.x issues in my project.

title: Tail Recursion does not work
author: DrDub

title: Add iterable to the method definitions
author: mschop
body:
Hi,

since PHP 7.1 one can use `iterable` type hint for everything that is iterable, including array and Traversable.

I know this lib shall also work for older PHP version, but adding iterable to the phpdoc-block would not hurt.

Currently IDE's and other tools throw warnings when I pass an iterable instead of an array or a Traversable.

Suggestion:

Add iterable to all doc-blocks.

Best Regards
Martin

title: `take_left(sequence_linear(date('Y') - 3, 1), 3)` infinite loop
author: Maxdw
body: Expected take_left to support iterators without requiring fully iterating through it. Because sequence_linear returns an infinite iterator the take_left function is never able to provide the first $count of iteration values due to its use of array_to_iterator.

title: Use php-quickcheck for testing
author: lstrojny
body:
Generative testing looks ideal for Functional PHP and https://github.com/steos/php-quickcheck looks like a good implementation.

### TBD
- [ ] weither to use it inside of PHPUnit or not. First instinct is rather not but the question is weither it's sensible to port tests like `FunctionalTest` and `AnnotationsTest`

title: When will the php v8 be supported?
author: Yangeok

title: Proposal: Add compact()
author: phanan
body: WDYT about adding a `Functional\compact()` function that removes all falsy values from a collection [à la lodash](https://lodash.com/docs/4.17.15#compact)? It can be named something like `compress()` as well if there's any concern regarding the existing `compact()` in PHP. I can give it a try.

title: PHP 8 Support
author: acelaya
body:
Hello.

Is there any plans to support PHP 8? I can try to help if so.

title: [BUG] 1.14.0 breaks when opcache.preload is enabled
author: danfraticiu
body:
Our system just crashed and burned in producation with this error after `composer update` install 1.14 

```
Fatal error: Cannot redeclare Functional\match() (previously declared in /srv/app/vendor/lstrojny/functional-php/src/Functional/Matching.php(46) : eval()’d code:3) in /srv/app/vendor/lstrojny/functional-php/src/Functional/Matching.php(46) : eval()’d code on line 3
```

We haven't looked much into it, but disabling opcache.preload fixed the issue. For now we will downgrade to 1.12.0.

Not sure what the reasoning for the `eval` block was, but I'm pretty sure it's not needed it as you could declare the function inside the `if`

ex: https://3v4l.org/Y8Ulb

LE: I can send a PR tomorrow if needed.

title: Functions can no longer be called `match` on PHP 8
author: timfennis
body:
Since all functions are loaded by default projects using this library can not run on PHP 8 even when the `match` function is never used.

Since the solution is has to entail a BC break I'm assuming this has to be moved to version 2.0, although it would be great if there could be a PHP 8 compatible release without having to deal with even more BC-breaks.

title: Make these functions more expressive
author: videni

title: pick should use array_key_exists instead of isset
author: lorenzomar
body: pick source code use `isset` to check if the given key is present in the collection. This implies that  NULL values are not valid but they should be. What about to use array_key_exists?

title: Let the default callback argument to function some, none, and every be the identity function
author: KJSanchez
body: Bet this would be helpful when you have just an array of booleans.

title: Support psalm
author: lstrojny
body:
Add psalm annotations
 - [ ] Fix all issues found by psalm
 - [x] Increase type specificity
 - [x] Add `@psalm-pure`
 - [x] Add `@psalm-assert`
 - [ ] Add proper support for more complex cases like `compose`

### Type specificity
 - [x] Average
 - [x] ButLast

 - [x] DropLast
 - [x] Each
 - [x] Equal

 - [x] Every
 - [x] Exceptions/InvalidArgumentException
 - [x] Exceptions/MatchException
 - [x] False
 - [x] Falsy

 - [x] First

 - [x] Flatten
 - [x] Flip
 - [x] Functional
 - [x] GreaterThan

 - [x] Head
 - [x] Id
 - [x] Identical
 - [x] IfElse
 - [x] IndexesOf
 - [x] Intersperse
 - [x] Invoke

 - [x] InvokeIf
 - [x] InvokeLast
 - [x] Invoker
 - [x] Last

 - [x] LessThan

 - [x] LexicographicCompare
 - [x] Map
 - [x] Match
 - [x] Maximum
 - [x] Memoize
 - [x] Minimum
 - [x] None
 - [x] Noop
 - [x] Not
 - [x] OmitKeys
 - [x] PartialAny
 - [x] PartialLeft
 - [x] PartialMethod
 - [x] PartialRight
 - [x] Partition
 - [x] Pick
 - [x] Pluck
 - [x] Poll
 - [x] Product
 - [x] Ratio
 - [x] ReduceLeft
 - [x] ReduceRight
 - [x] Reindex
 - [x] Reject
 - [x] Repeat
 - [x] Retry
 - [x] Select
 - [x] SelectKeys
 - [x] SequenceConstant
 - [x] SequenceExponential
 - [x] SequenceLinear

### Issues
 - invoker(), invoke(), partial_method(): no way to annotate method names properly: https://github.com/vimeo/psalm/issues/2500
 - Recursive types are not implemented (problem for `compose`, `curry`, etc.): https://github.com/vimeo/psalm/issues/2499

title: The opposite of `select_keys`
author: jasonmm
body:
The [`select_keys` function](https://github.com/lstrojny/functional-php/blob/master/docs/functional-php.md#select_keys) is extremely handy. However, sometimes I only know which keys I don't want, or the number of keys I don't want is significantly smaller than those I do want. I did not see a function (for example, `remove_keys`) that would allow for that.

Is it expected that key removal be accomplished with other functions? Would you be interested in a PR for a `remove_keys` function?

title: Porper file comment instead of full license text
author: szepeviktor
body:
Are you open to replace this https://github.com/lstrojny/functional-php/blob/master/src/Functional/Contains.php#L2-L22 with proper file comment?

```php
/**
 * @package   Functional-php
 * @author    Lars Strojny <lstrojny@php.net>
 * @copyright 2011-2017 Lars Strojny
 * @license   https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT MIT
 * @link      https://github.com/lstrojny/functional-php
 */
```

https://docs.phpdoc.org/references/phpdoc/basic-syntax.html

Hi,

title: PHP 7?
author: phanan
body: The support for PHP 5.6 (and even 7.0) is ending by [the end of this year](https://secure.php.net/supported-versions.php). Should we make the lib PHP 7.1? Happy to work on it if positive.
